title: Postpartum Pelvic Floor Recovery Journal
subtitle: 12-Week Daily Tracker for New Moms - Kegels, Leakage, Prolapse, Pain & Pelvic PT Visit Prep
author: Deokgu Studio
description:
Most postpartum care ends at the 6-week visit - but pelvic floor recovery doesn't. This 12-week daily journal is built for the fourth trimester, the months when leakage, prolapse heaviness, and perineal or C-section pain are most plastic - and most often dismissed.

Who it's for:
- New moms in the first 12 weeks postpartum (vaginal birth, C-section, VBAC)
- Patients preparing for OB, midwife, or pelvic-floor PT visits
- Anyone tracking Kegels, leakage, or prolapse symptoms after birth
- Baby-shower gift for any expecting mom who wants real recovery support

What's inside:
- Daily symptom log: leakage, urge, bowel, prolapse, perineum/incision pain (0-5)
- Daily Kegel table: long holds, quick flicks, how it felt
- Weekly review with leakage trend graph
- 4 Pelvic Floor PT visit prep pages (every 3 weeks)
- Recovery milestones reference (week 1 through beyond 12)
- Kegel & symptom guide + red-flag list
- Baseline (Day 1) + 12-week checkpoint comparison

This is data, by you, about you - the kind that turns the 6-week brush-off into a real referral.
categories: BISAC: HEALTH & FITNESS / Women's Health / Postnatal
BISAC: FAMILY & RELATIONSHIPS / Babies & Toddlers / Postnatal Care
